title: ICE Arrests of Immigrants Without Criminal Convictions Up 770% Under Trump — NBC/TRAC Analysis
detail: An analysis published on April 8, 2026 found that ICE arrests of immigrants without criminal convictions increased 770% during the first year of Trump's second term, with street arrests surging over 1,000%. TRAC Reports data from April 4 showed 70.8% of ICE detainees (42,722 of 60,311) had no criminal conviction. NBC News reported that two-thirds of the nearly 400,000 ICE arrests recorded since January 2025 involved individuals without any criminal convictions, and 38% had neither criminal convictions nor pending criminal charges. This came even after administration officials signaled a desire to focus enforcement on serious criminals following the deaths of two U.S. citizens during Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota.

media.caption: ICE arrests of immigrants without criminal records surge 770% under Trump

title: Trump Admin Reaffirms Plan to Deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia Despite Court Ruling
detail: The Trump administration reaffirmed its plans to deport Kilmar Abrego Garcia — a Salvadoran man the government previously admitted was erroneously deported to El Salvador in violation of a court order — this time to Liberia, a day after Judge Paula Xinis rejected the Liberia plan and rebuked the DOJ. The case has become one of the highest-profile wrongful deportation cases in recent U.S. history. A federal judge ordered his return to the U.S. and the Supreme Court unanimously upheld that order, but the administration has refused to comply, instead announcing a new deportation target of Liberia despite Abrego Garcia having no known ties to that country. Legal experts and advocates warn the administration's stance represents a direct defiance of court orders and could set a dangerous precedent for due process rights in immigration enforcement. Abrego Garcia's lawyers filed a motion to dismiss the pending human smuggling charges as vindictive prosecution.

media.caption: Trump administration reaffirms plan to deport Abrego Garcia to Liberia

title: ICE Labeled 1,300 Operation Metro Surge Arrests as 'Collateral' — 34% of Total Were Untargeted
detail: New reporting published April 8, 2026 revealed that ICE labeled over 1,300 of the approximately 3,785 total arrests made during Operation Metro Surge in Minnesota as 'collateral' arrests — meaning those individuals were not the original targets of the operation but were arrested because they happened to be present during enforcement actions targeting other people. This represented roughly 34% of total Metro Surge arrests. The disclosure came from FOIA data and internal ICE records reviewed by the Minnesota Reformer and other outlets. Advocacy groups pointed to the collateral arrest data as evidence that the operation was not the targeted criminal enforcement operation the Trump administration had publicly described. The 'collateral' framing echoed earlier revelations that fewer than 25% of Metro Surge arrestees had any criminal record. The operation, which was underway from late December 2025 through late March 2026, resulted in over 1,400 peak daily removals before the administration began pulling back following public backlash.

media.caption: ICE labeled over 1,300 Operation Metro Surge arrests as 'collateral'
